items.title: # Outline Presentation Flow
items.content: Act as a presentation expert. Create a detailed 10-slide outline for a presentation on [Topic] for a [Audience]. Include key points for each slide, a clear introduction, and a strong conclusion.

items.title: # Develop Slide Content
items.content: Write the full content for a single presentation slide on [Specific Point of Topic]. The audience is [Audience]. Focus on clarity, conciseness, and engaging bullet points. Include a title.

items.title: # Write Engaging Intro
items.content: Draft a compelling 1-minute introduction speech for a presentation about [Topic]. The goal is to capture the attention of [Audience] and clearly state the presentation's value. Make it impactful.

items.title: # Quick Meeting Recap
items.content: Summarize the following meeting transcript. Identify key decisions, action items with owners and deadlines, and any open questions. Format as bullet points for easy reading. Transcript: [Paste meeting transcript here]

items.title: # Executive Briefing Digest
items.content: You are a senior analyst. Summarize the following meeting transcript for an executive audience. Focus on high-level outcomes, strategic implications, and next steps that require executive attention. Keep the summary concise. Transcript: [Paste meeting transcript here]

items.title: # Meeting Follow-up Email
items.content: Draft a follow-up email to attendees summarizing our recent meeting. Include the main topic, key decisions made, action items with owners and deadlines, and a polite closing. The tone should be professional and concise. Meeting details: [Paste meeting summary or transcript snippets here]. Attendees: [List attendees]

items.title: # Quick Apology Reply
items.content: Write a friendly apology email for a customer who received a delayed order. Include: sincere sorry, brief reason, new delivery date, and a small discount code. Keep tone warm, professional, and under 150 words.

items.title: # Friendly FAQ Answer
items.content: Create a short chat reply that explains how to reset a forgotten password. Use simple steps, mention where to click, and reassure the user it’s safe. Limit to three sentences and keep the tone helpful.

items.title: # Positive Upsell Message
items.content: Draft a polite response to a customer who just bought a basic plan, suggesting they upgrade to premium for extra features. Highlight two key benefits, add a limited‑time free trial, and thank them for their purchase.

items.title: # Viral Product Launch
items.content: Create a step‑by‑step marketing campaign for a new tech gadget. Include 3 social media platforms, 2 email ideas, a launch event concept, and a budget outline. Use simple language and give clear actions for a small team.

items.title: # Seasonal Sale Boost
items.content: Design a 4‑week promotional plan for a summer clothing sale. List weekly themes, 3 Instagram post ideas, a discount code strategy, and a short ad copy. Keep the plan easy to follow and suitable for a boutique store.

items.title: # Brand Storytelling Sprint
items.content: Write a quick brand storytelling campaign for a coffee shop. Provide a core story hook, 2 blog post outlines, 3 Instagram Reel scripts, and a loyalty program teaser. Make each piece short, friendly, and ready to publish.

items.title: # Spy Market Moves
items.content: List the top 5 competitors in my industry, summarize their latest product releases, pricing, and marketing messages. Highlight any new features or promotions they launched in the past 3 months and note gaps I could exploit.

items.title: # Beat Their SEO
items.content: Analyze the SEO strengths of my three main rivals. Provide their top 10 ranking keywords, backlink count, and content topics. Suggest three easy-to-rank keywords I can target and two content ideas to outrank them.

items.title: # Social Sentiment Scan
items.content: Gather recent social media mentions of my two biggest competitors. Summarize common customer complaints and praises in bullet points. Identify one area where they are weak and propose a quick improvement for my brand.

items.title: # Spy On Rivals
items.content: List the top 5 competitors in my market. For each, give their main product, pricing, key strengths, weaknesses, and recent marketing moves. Summarize this in a table and add a short suggestion on how I can differentiate my offering.

items.title: # Benchmark Feature Gaps
items.content: Compare my product's features with those of the three leading competitors. Mark which features I have, lack, or exceed. Highlight the biggest gaps and suggest one quick improvement to close each gap.

items.title: # Social Sentiment Scan
items.content: Gather recent customer comments about my top three rivals from social media and review sites. Identify the most common praise and complaints for each. Provide a 2‑sentence insight on what customers value most and what they dislike.

items.title: # Spot Trends Fast
items.content: You are a market analyst. List the top five emerging consumer trends in the US for the next 12 months, include brief data sources, why they matter, and a simple action each brand can take to profit.

items.title: # Competitor Gap Finder
items.content: Act as a competitive researcher. Compare three main rivals in the sustainable fashion market on price, product range, marketing channels, and customer sentiment. Highlight one clear gap each competitor leaves open for a new entrant.

items.title: # Survey Insight Builder
items.content: Create a short online survey (8 questions) to gauge interest in a subscription box for home‑cooking kits. Include two demographic questions, three preference questions, and three willingness‑to‑pay questions. Add a brief intro and thank‑you note.
